mission.title: longhorn: [IMPROVEMENT] Metrics for backups
mission.description: [IMPROVEMENT] Metrics for backups. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current longhorn deployment
steps.description:
Verify your longhorn version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
helm list -n longhorn 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working longhorn installation.

steps.title: Review longhorn configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant longhorn configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
kubectl get configmap -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=longhorn
```
### Is your improvement request related to a feature? Please describe (👍 if you like this request)

Collect the metrics of backups, record them in the CRs, and expose them on Longhorn manager.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [IMPROVEMENT] Metrics for backups
steps.description:
Tested on master-head (longhorn-manager d75a8a1) by following [the test steps](https://github.com/longhorn/longhorn/issues/11387#issuecomment-3808966735).

`longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count` can be found in the metrics:

```
# curl -s http://10.42.1.6:9500/metrics | grep
```yaml
# curl -s http://10.42.1.6:9500/metrics | grep longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count
# HELP longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count Number of backup volumes on this backup target
# TYPE longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count gauge
longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count{backup_target="default"} 1
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
kubectl get events -n longhorn --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[IMPROVEMENT] Metrics for backups" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Tested on master-head (longhorn-manager d75a8a1) by following [the test steps](https://github.com/longhorn/longhorn/issues/11387#issuecomment-3808966735).
codeSnippets:
# curl -s http://10.42.1.6:9500/metrics | grep longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count
# HELP longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count Number of backup volumes on this backup target
# TYPE longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count gauge
longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count{backup_target="default"} 1

# curl -s http://10.42.1.6:9500/metrics | grep longhorn_backup_volume_backups_count
# empty 

# curl -s http://10.42.1.6:9500/metrics | grep longhorn_backup_accumulated_physical_file_size_bytes
# empty

$ kubectl exec -it longhorn-manager-ptclb -n longhorn-system -- /bin/sh
sh-4.4# curl -s http://10.42.1.6:9500/metrics | grep longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count
# HELP longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count Number of backup volumes on this backup target
# TYPE longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count gauge
longhorn_backup_target_backup_volume_count{backup_target="default"} 2

sh-4.4# curl -s http://10.42.1.6:9500/metrics | grep longhorn_backup_volume_backups_count      
# HELP longhorn_backup_volume_backups_count Number of backups belonging to this backup volume
# TYPE longhorn_backup_volume_backups_count gauge
longhorn_backup_volume_backups_count{backup_volume="vol-2-280cc385"} 1
longhorn_backup_volume_backups_count{backup_volume="vol-c4eb6c05"} 1

sh-4.4# curl -s http://10.4

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with longhorn installed or the issue environment reproducible.
